data.title: Stanley Black & Decker Authorizes $500M Share Repurchase
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Stanley Black & Decker approved a $500 million share repurchase authorization.

The power and hand tools maker also terminated its previous repurchase authorization of up to 20 million shares as part of the new repurchase program structure, the company said Friday. The termination took effect April 23, and at the time, all 20 million shares remained available under the prior authorization.

The new authorization will expire three years from the date of approval, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: First Capital reports Q1 net income $4.3M; diluted EPS $1.30, NIM 3.81%
data.description:
First Capital reported net income of $4.3 million, or $1.30 per diluted share, for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, up from $3.2 million, or $0.97 per diluted share, in Q1 2025. Net interest income after provision for credit losses rose as higher yields and larger average interest-earning asset balances drove interest income, while interest expense declined. The company also saw higher noninterest income and increased noninterest expenses year-over-year.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $4.333 million for Q1 2026 versus $3.238 million for Q1 2025; net income attributable to First Capital: $4.330 million vs. $3.235 million.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.30 for Q1 2026 compared with $0.97 for Q1 2025.
  • Total interest income: $14.924 million for Q1 2026 versus $13.346 million for Q1 2025; total interest expense: $3.506 million versus $3.765 million.
  • Net interest income: $11.418 million for Q1 2026 versus $9.581 million for Q1 2025; tax-equivalent net interest margin: 3.81% vs. 3.34%.
  • Total noninterest income: $2.048 million for Q1 2026 versus $1.848 million for Q1 2025; total noninterest expense: $7.753 million versus $7.181 million.

Business Highlights

  • Average interest-earning assets increased to $1.221 billion in Q1 2026 from $1.169 billion in Q1 2025, supporting higher interest income.
  • Management sold $18.7 million of available-for-sale securities to reposition the investment portfolio for increased future yields, recognizing a $92,000 loss on sale in the quarter.
  • Provision for credit losses was $350,000 in Q1 2026, with net charge-offs of $111,000; allowance for credit losses represented 1.53% of gross loans at March 31, 2026.
  • Deposits increased to $1.137 billion at March 31, 2026 from $1.123 billion at December 31, 2025, and nonperforming assets decreased to $4.0 million.
  • The Bank operates 17 offices across Indiana and Kentucky and continues to offer online banking and electronic bill payment access via its website.

Original SEC Filing: FIRST CAPITAL INC [ FCAP ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: AVAX Announces 10 MW Tier 3 AI/HPC Site in Alberta; Q1 Revenue Doubles to $2.4M, $27.2M Cash
data.description:
AVAX reported preliminary Q1 2026 revenue of approximately $2.4 million and executed an LOI to develop an initial 10 MW Tier 3 AI/HPC powered land site in Alberta.

Key Highlights:

  • Executed LOI with BlueFlare to develop 10 MW Tier 3-ready powered land near Calgary; definitive agreement to be signed within 30 days.
  • Target delivery: powered land ready for end-client deployment in Q1 2027; design prioritizes brownfield natural gas generation and Tier 3 resilience.
  • Preliminary Q1 2026 revenue more than doubled sequentially to ~$2.4M, driven by Avalanche staking rewards and Bitcoin mining.
  • Cash balance of $27.2M, projected to fund operations for over three years without liquidating digital assets.
  • Full-year 2026 guidance reiterated: revenue $11M–$12M and EBITDA $2M–$3M at current spot prices; project cost estimated $30–$35M.

Original SEC Filing: AVAX ONE TECHNOLOGY LTD. [ AVX ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: ADT reports limited cloud access; says incident not expected to be material
data.description:
ADT reported limited unauthorized access to cloud environments and does not expect a material financial impact.

Key Highlights:

  • Discovered unauthorized access to certain cloud-based environments on April 20, 2026; access was promptly terminated.
  • Activated its incident response plan, launched an investigation and engaged third-party cybersecurity experts.
  • Determined only limited customer and prospective customer data was accessed based on the investigation.
  • Company does not believe the incident is reasonably likely to have a material impact on financial condition or operations.

Original SEC Filing: ADT Inc. [ ADT ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: Intel's stock extends its spectacular run by posting its best day in nearly four decades
data.description:
By Emily Bary and Hannah Pedone

There's a debate on Wall Street about whether Intel's financial prospects have materially changed

Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan "fixed the balance sheet," according to an analyst.

Have things fundamentally changed for Intel, or is the stock simply being fueled by vibes?

That's a hot topic of debate on Wall Street, as some analysts argue that the boom in server central processing units has dramatically altered the financial picture for Intel (INTC). Others say a rosy narrative is masking persistent concerns about Intel's fundamentals.

Intel's stock is surging 26.1% in morning trading on Friday. If the move holds through the close, it would translate to Intel's largest one-day gain since Oct. 29, 1987, when it rose 26.4% according to Dow Jones Market Data. Shares are up 130% on the year to rank third in the S&P 500 over that span.

At least one analyst just changed his mind about Intel's prospects. "It has been easy to not like" Intel, Evercore ISI's Mark Lipacis wrote in a note to clients. The company had been facing a laundry list of potentially existential issues, like repeated technological missteps and an erosion of market share.

But three things have since changed for Intel, according to Lipacis, who upgraded the stock to outperform from in-line following Thursday afternoon's earnings report. For one, "the fastest-growing AI workloads need a lot more CPUs." Whereas AI workloads once required one CPU for every eight graphics processing units, that ratio could completely flip to eight CPUs for every GPU, he explained.

Read: Intel earnings send the stock soaring. Here's what you need to know.

Additionally, CEO Lip-Bu Tan "fixed the balance sheet" as Intel's technology is more competitive, according to Lipacis. And the company's manufacturing business is suddenly more attractive as the U.S. government backs domestic chip-making ventures.

Seaport analyst Jay Goldberg said he thinks the company is on its "firmest footing in years."

The company's recent announcement of its Terafab semiconductor plant, jointly developed by Tesla (TSLA), xAI, SpaceX and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has been validating for investors, he said, and he expects further announcements later this year.

Goldberg added that, while the company still faces constraints on capacity for CPU production, he expects them to ease throughout this year.

Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis argued that the AI opportunity has made Intel's other issues fade into the background. "We continue to believe it remains early days for AI's impact on the CPU market with plenty of room to run," he wrote in a note to clients. Explosive growth in the usage of accelerated processors "is driving real server CPU demand."

That said, he kept a hold rating on Intel's stock as he waits to see more traction from large customers and for the company's foundry business.

Bernstein's Stacy Rasgon stayed on the sidelines as well. While "narrative/headlines may fuel the vibe for now," he said, elements within Intel's report might resonate with bearish investors.

"Namely while 18A yields are seemingly running better than expected they apparently remain underwhelming," Rasgon wrote, referring to the portion of usable chips that Intel gets from its manufacturing technology. And the company faces various cost pressures, such that even when Rasgon increases his revenue expectations "fairly decently," his profit projections barely budge.

Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore was also cautious. He said that the "vast majority" of the company's CPUs are not in data centers, so AI growth will only apply to a portion of its CPU volumes.

He also expressed concerns over the nature of the Terafab project, saying that the structure of the collaboration isn't clear.

-Emily Bary -Hannah Pedone

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Intel Chief Accounting Officer Gawel Resigns
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Intel's chief accounting officer, Scott Gawel, resigned Friday to pursue another professional opportunity, effective immediately.

Chief Financial Officer David Zinsner assumed the role of principal accounting officer, Intel said. The company disclosed the changes in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Gawel's exit comes a day after the company reported 7% sales growth in the first quarter and lifted its second-quarter outlook above Wall Street consensus, citing strong demand for its CPUs. Intel shares closed up 24% to $82.54 on Friday.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Tech boosts US stocks to record close, oil dips on signs of Iran peace progress
data.description:
Key points:
  • Markets choppy amid peace talk hopes and corporate earnings
  • S&P 500, Nasdaq notch modest weekly gains; Dow ticks lower on the week
  • WTI settles down 1.51%, Brent settles down 0.25%
  • Dollar posts weekly gain

By Stephen Culp

The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq notched all-time closing highs on Friday as investors assessed signs of progress in U.S.-Iran peace talks and weighed solid corporate earnings against downbeat forward guidance due to war-related energy price shocks.

Tech shares, buoyed by strong results from Intel , helped to lift the Nasdaq 1.6%, while the S&P 500's gain was more modest. The Dow closed in negative territory.

For the week, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq logged modest gains, while the blue-chip Dow registered a decline from last Friday's close.

"Intel just confirms the AI boom is alive and well, and this earnings season is off to a tremendous start," said Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group in Omaha.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi arrived in Pakistan to discuss proposals to restart peace talks between the United States and Iran. U.S. President Donald Trump told Reuters that Iran plans to make an offer aimed at meeting U.S. demands.

"There's optimism that talks are going to restart soon," Detrick added. "With the extreme weakness we've seen from defense stocks the last two weeks, the market might be saying there could be some type of major peace deal on the horizon, which clearly is a net positive."

Oil prices dipped as optimism over potential U.S.-Iran peace talks offset supply concerns, but were sharply higher on the week.

U.S. crude fell 1.51% to settle at $94.40 per barrel, while Brent settled at $105.33 per barrel, down 0.25% on the day.

SOLID CORPORATE EARNINGS MARRED BY DOUR GUIDANCE

First-quarter reporting season has hit full stride, with 139 of the companies in the S&P 500 having reported. Of those, 81% have beaten earnings estimates. Analysts now see aggregate year-on-year S&P 500 earnings growth of 16.1%, up from the 14.4% growth projected at the beginning of the quarter, according to LSEG I/B/E/S.

But on analyst conference calls, CEOs are increasingly providing downbeat guidance due to spiking fuel costs resulting from the war with Iran. Consumer products company Procter & Gamble warned on its Friday earnings call that it expects a roughly $1 billion hit to its fiscal 2027 profit because of the war-related surge in energy prices.

Several high-profile earnings are on the docket next week, including tech and tech-adjacent megacaps Amazon , Alphabet , and Meta Platforms . Oil supermajors Exxon Mobil and Chevron are slated to report next Friday.

"There's some very big names set to report next week," Detrick said. "We're optimistic we're going to continue to see solid earnings, just like we have had so far this earnings season."

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 79.61 points, or 0.16%, to 49,230.71, the S&P 500 rose 56.68 points, or 0.80%, to 7,165.08, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 398.09 points, or 1.63%, to 24,836.60.

European shares closed lower, dipping to a more than two-week low and logging a weekly decline of 2.5% as investors worried about the disruption of energy supplies by the Middle East conflict.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 5.42 points, or 0.51%, to 1,072.73.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index fell 0.58%, while Europe's broad FTSEurofirst 300 index fell 12.54 points, or 0.51%.

Emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS rose 12.86 points, or 0.80%, to 1,612.17. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) closed higher by 0.81%, at 825.78, while Japan's Nikkei rose 575.95 points, or 0.97%, to 59,716.18.

The dollar dipped but remained on pace for a weekly gain as war uncertainty kept investors on edge.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, fell 0.32% to 98.51, with the euro up 0.3% at $1.1718.

Against the Japanese yen , the dollar weakened 0.19% to 159.4.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin fell 0.43% to $77,587.82. Ethereum declined 0.27% to $2,320.06.

U.S. Treasury yields dipped on hopes that U.S.-Iran peace talks could take place over the weekend, bolstering confidence that the war could be close to ending.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell 1.7 basis points to 4.306%, from 4.323% late on Thursday.

The 30-year bond (US30YT=RR) yield fell 0.3 basis point to 4.9153% from 4.918% late on Thursday.

The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, fell 4.5 basis points to 3.78%, from 3.825% late on Thursday.

Gold advanced on the day but registered a weekly loss, as war-related inflation worries persisted.

Spot gold rose 0.38% to $4,710.30 an ounce. U.S. gold futures (GCc1) rose 0.2% to $4,714.40 an ounce.

data.title: S&P Cuts Slovakia's Sovereign Debt Rating on Weaker Economic Growth, High Spending
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

S&P Global Ratings cut Slovakia's sovereign credit rating, citing projections for slower economic growth and high spending.

The ratings agency on Friday cut Slovakia's rating to A from A+. The outlook is stable.

S&P projects Slovakia's economic growth will slow to 0.5% this year, constrained by both domestic and external factors. Domestic consumption is being weighed down by weak consumer confidence and fiscal consolidation, while geopolitical and economic uncertainty is limiting export demand, the ratings agency said.

S&P forecasts Slovakia's economic growth will recover to an average of 1.9% in 2027 through 2029, but said that rate is still modest.

Additionally, Slovakia's budgetary consolidation will likely be slower than previously expected given high spending on social transfers, defense and the upcoming parliamentary elections, S&P said.

"Given the rigidity of spending, we now expect the fiscal deficit to remain elevated for longer, keeping net general government debt on an upward path," the ratings agency said.

S&P now projects Slovakia's general government fiscal deficit will average 4.8% of GDP over the next few years, pushing net government debt to 62% in 2029, or about 20 percentage points above its pre-pandemic level.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Celestica Inc expected to post earnings of $2.08 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Celestica Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 27 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Toronto Ontario-based company is expected to report a 50.9% increase in revenue to $3.996 billion from $2.65 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 16 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on January 28 2026, for the period ended March 31, was for revenue between $3.85 billion and $4.15 billion. (Majority Basis is on Total Revenue)

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Celestica Inc is for earnings of $2.08 per share. The company's EPS guidance on January 28 2026, for the period ended March 31, was between $1.95 and $2.15

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 19 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.0% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Celestica Inc is $412.50, about 5.3% above its last closing price of $391.60

The company's guidance on January 28 2026 for the period ended March 31 was for Earnings before Interest and Taxes between USD300.3 million and USD323.7 million.
